name: microsoft-mailtools-mcp-foundry
title: Work IQ Mail MCP Server
summary: Comprehensive tools for email operations including create, send, reply, update, delete, and...
description: Comprehensive tools for email operations including create, send, reply, update, delete, and search messages with optional file attachments.

useCases.name: AddDraftAttachments
useCases.description: Add attachments (URI) to an existing draft message.

useCases.name: UpdateDraft
useCases.description: Update a draft's recipients, subject, body, sensitivity, and attachments. Supports both file URIs (OneDrive/SharePoint) and direct file uploads (base64-encoded). Recipients can be provided as names or email addresses - names will be automatically resolved to email addresses using Microsoft Graph.

useCases.name: SendEmailWithAttachments
useCases.description: Create and send an email with optional attachments. Supports both file URIs (OneDrive/SharePoint) and direct file uploads (base64-encoded). Recipients can be provided as names or email addresses - names will be automatically resolved to email addresses using Microsoft Graph.

useCases.name: CreateDraftMessage
useCases.description: Create a draft email in the signed-in user's mailbox without sending it. Recipients can be provided as names or email addresses - names will be automatically resolved to email addresses using Microsoft Graph.

useCases.name: GetMessage
useCases.description: Get a message by ID from the signed-in user's mailbox.

useCases.name: UpdateMessage
useCases.description: Update a message's mutable properties (subject, body, categories, importance, sensitivity).

useCases.name: FlagEmail
useCases.description: Update the flag status on an email message. Supports flagging, completing, or clearing a flag.

useCases.name: DeleteMessage
useCases.description: Delete a message from the signed-in user's mailbox.

useCases.name: ReplyToMessage
useCases.description: Reply to an existing message. By default creates a reply draft. Set sendImmediately=true to send the reply immediately instead of creating a draft. Recipients can be provided as names or email addresses - names will be automatically resolved using Microsoft Graph.

useCases.name: ReplyAllToMessage
useCases.description: Reply-all to an existing message. By default creates a reply-all draft. Set sendImmediately=true to send the reply-all immediately instead of creating a draft. Recipients can be provided as names or email addresses - names will be automatically resolved using Microsoft Graph.

useCases.name: SendDraftMessage
useCases.description: Send an existing draft message by ID.

useCases.name: SearchMessages
useCases.description: If the user's prompt can be deterministically expressed as OData filters or using KQL search syntax, always try SearchMessagesQueryParameters first as it is faster and does not have indexing delays. Use SearchMessages only when the query requires interpretation, relevance ranking, or intent understanding that cannot be expressed deterministically using OData filters (e.g., 'most urgent emails from last week', 'messages needing my response'). SearchMessages uses natural language powered by Microsoft 365 Copilot. Can be slow (up to 30 seconds) and recent messages may not appear immediately due to indexing delay.

useCases.name: SearchMessagesQueryParameters
useCases.description: Searches the current user's mailbox for email messages by passing OData query parameters directly to https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/messages. This tool does not interpret, rank, or infer intent. IMPORTANT RESTRICTIONS: (1) $search performs keyword matching and supports KQL syntax (from:, to:, subject:, cc:, bcc:). It CANNOT be combined with $filter, $orderBy, or $skip. Use $search alone: '?$search="from:alice subject:budget"'. (2) $filter supported properties: subject, from/emailAddress/address, sender/emailAddress/address, receivedDateTime, isRead, isDraft, hasAttachments, importance, parentFolderId, categories. NOT filterable: body, bodyPreview, displayName, folder. COLLECTION PROPERTIES NOT FILTERABLE: toRecipients, ccRecipients, bccRecipients are collections and cannot be used in $filter (neither direct path nor any()/all()). To search by recipient, use $search instead: '?$search="to:user@example.com"'. (3) Combining $filter with $orderby may fail with 'InefficientFilter'. Prefer $filter alone and sort results client-side. EXAMPLES: '?$filter=isRead eq false', '?$filter=receivedDateTime ge 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z&$top=25'. PAGING: When the response contains hasMoreResults: true, call this tool again passing the nextLink value as the nextLink parameter to retrieve the next page.

useCases.name: GetAttachments
useCases.description: Get all attachments from a message, returning attachment metadata (ID, name, size, type).

useCases.name: DownloadAttachment
useCases.description: Download attachment content from a message. Returns the content as base64-encoded string.

useCases.name: UploadAttachment
useCases.description: Upload a small file attachment (less than 3 MB) to a message. File content must be base64-encoded.

useCases.name: UploadLargeAttachment
useCases.description: Upload a large file attachment (3-150 MB) to a message using chunked upload. File content must be base64-encoded.

useCases.name: DeleteAttachment
useCases.description: Delete an attachment from a message.

useCases.name: ReplyWithFullThread
useCases.description: Reply (or reply-all) adding new recipients while preserving full quoted thread and optionally re-attaching original files. By default creates a reply draft. Set sendImmediately=true to send the reply immediately instead of creating a draft. Recipients can be provided as names or email addresses - names will be automatically resolved using Microsoft Graph.

useCases.name: ReplyAllWithFullThread
useCases.description: Reply-all adding new recipients while preserving full quoted thread and optionally re-attaching original files. By default creates a reply-all draft. Set sendImmediately=true to send immediately.

useCases.name: ForwardMessageWithFullThread
useCases.description: Forward a message adding new recipients and an optional intro comment while preserving full quoted thread; returns sensitivity label. Recipients can be provided as names or email addresses - names will be automatically resolved using Microsoft Graph.

useCases.name: ForwardMessage
useCases.description: Forward an existing message, optionally adding a comment, recipients, and new attachments while preserving the quoted thread. Recipients can be provided as names or email addresses - names will be automatically resolved using Microsoft Graph.

mcpMailToolsOAuth2.description: Authenticate with Work IQ using OAuth2 authorization code flow with PKCE support.
